channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: What would it cost England and Scotland fans to go to World Cup?
item.description: High ticket prices, expensive accommodation and train fares way above normal rates. If you want to book a World Cup trip now, this is what it would cost.

item.title: Is it advantage Arsenal in dramatic title race?
item.description: Are Arsenal now firmly in pole position to be Premier League champions after the Gunners went six points clear of Manchester City with a 3-0 win over Fulham.

item.title: Has much-maligned Gyokeres proved his critics wrong?
item.description: The talk before the season was that Arsenal needed a 20-goal-a-season striker to win a trophy - and now they finally have one in Viktor Gyokeres.

item.title: Woodwork woe and a Spurs lifeline - West Ham suffer in survival fight
item.description: Tottenham were the biggest winners as fellow Premier League strugglers West Ham hit the woodwork four times in their 3-0 defeat at Brentford.

item.title: Was season a missed opportunity for Wrexham?
item.description: After finishing seventh in the Championship, BBC Sport looks at whether or not Wrexham will see the 2025-26 season as a missed opportunity.

item.title: VAR, fine margins & 'superior' Lyon cost Arsenal Champions League final spot
item.description: Arsenal eliminated from the Champions League by Lyon after a 4-3 aggregrate defeat and an eventful match in France - with VAR heavily involved throughout

item.title: Arsenal beat Fulham to move six points clear of Man City
item.description: Watch highlights as Arsenal beat Fulham 3-0 to move six points clear of Manchester City at the top of the Premier League.

item.title: 'It's ridiculous - the rule has to change' - Rooney
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Wayne Rooney and Joe Hart debate whether all hair pulls are worthy of a red card following another incident where Sunderland's Dan Ballard was sent off.

item.title: Three red cards for hair pulling in 2026 - is it time to change law?
item.description: Sunderland defender Dan Ballard becomes the third player to be sent off for pulling an opponent's hair in the Premier League this season. Is it time to change the law?

item.title: Liverpool target Napoli's Beukema - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Liverpool make offer for Dutch defender Sam Beukema, AC Milan offer Rafael Leao to Premier League sides, Manchester United target ex-Nottingham Forest midfielder Danilo, plus more.

item.title: 'I've never been part of a game like it' - how the play-off shootout unfolded
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at how the three-way race for the final Championship play-off spot unfolded between Hull City, Wrexham and Derby County.

item.title: West Ham survival hopes take a blow after defeat to Brentford
item.description: West Ham's Premier League survival hopes take a blow as they suffer a 3-0 defeat to Brentford at the Gtech Community Stadium.

item.title: Newcastle end losing streak with victory over Brighton
item.description: Newcastle hand manager Eddie Howe a timely boost as they pick up their first victory since March beating Brighton 3-1 at St. James' Park.

item.title: 'Fantastic all season' - Rooney on Gyokeres' impact
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Wayne Rooney analyses the impact Viktor Gyokeres has made at Arsenal as he reaches 20 goals for the season during their 3-0 victory over Fulham.

item.title: Why West Ham's defence could see them relegated
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Wayne Rooney analyses West Ham's poor defensive performance during their 3-0 defeat to Brentford.

item.title: Ballard sent off for hair pull in Sunderland draw at Wolves
item.description: Watch highlights as defender Dan Ballard is sent off for a hair pull on Tolu Arokodare during Wolves' 1-1 draw against Sunderland in the Premier League.

item.title: Brentford flying - but will sixth be good enough for Champions League?
item.description: Many predicted Brentford to go down. Instead they are on the cusp of securing European football next season.

item.title: Are Ipswich better equipped for Premier League this time?
item.description: Ipswich Town have won promotion from the Championship for the second time in three years - but are they better equipped for the Premier League in 2026?

item.title: Football awash with Guardiola exit talk as Man City plan for life without him
item.description: We will know in a matter of weeks whether Pep Guardiola's reign at Manchester City has run its course, writes Sami Mokbel.

item.title: Osula project pays off to help ease pressure on Howe
item.description: Newcastle United head coach Eddie Howe keeps faith with William Osula and the forward repays his faith in a crucial win against Brighton.

item.title: What went wrong for relegated Livingston?
item.description: What went wrong for Livingston as they are relegated from the Scottish Premiership for the second time in three seasons?

item.title: Credit for McKenna and Hull a play-off force - Sam Parkin's EFL takeaways
item.description: BBC Final Score Sam Parkin gives his take on the big EFL talking points as Ipswich win promotion and Hull make the play-offs.

item.title: I'm accountable for Man Utd results - Skinner
item.description: Manchester United boss Marc Skinner says he is accountable for results after home draw with Brighton makes it one win in nine.

item.title: Lyon beat Arsenal to reach Women's Champions League final
item.description: Arsenal's hopes of defending their Women's Champions League crown came to a crushing end as Jule Brand scores a dramatic 87th-minute winner to give Lyon a 4-3 semi-final win on aggregate.

item.title: Arsenal players showed hunger to win - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says the opportunity to win two competitions this season is fuelling his player's hunger to win matches after a commanding 3-0 victory over Fulham in the Premier League.

item.title: Schuller scores late equaliser as Man Utd earn draw against Brighton
item.description: Lea Schuller's injury-time equaliser denies Brighton a memorable victory at Leigh Sports Village but the draw means Manchester United's hopes of returning to the Champions League are virtually over.

item.title: 'He's taken a lot of stick' - Burn dedicates Brighton win to Howe
item.description: Newcastle United defender Dan Burn says it has been "tough" playing in both European and domestic football competitions, but his team are looking to end the season on a high after beating Brighton & Hove Albion in the Premier League.

item.title: Inverness win title & champions East Kilbride make history
item.description: Inverness Caledonian Thistle secure the Scottish League 1 title as East Kilbride create history by winning League 2.

item.title: Make or break for Rangers - what next in Scottish title race?
item.description: Rangers' travel to leaders Hearts with their hopes hanging by a thread - and can Celtic take care of hurting Hibernian?

item.title: Livingston relegated after one season in Premiership
item.description: Livingston are relegated from the Scottish Premiership after one season back in the top flight following Kilmarnock's 3-0 win over Dundee United.

item.title: Drama in their blood - Newport's latest escape act
item.description: Newport County's final-day win to seal League Two survival was the latest escape act from a club with a history of drama.

item.title: Penybont win Play-Off final to earn European spot
item.description: Chris Venables and Mael Davies score as Penybont beat Haverfordwest County 2-0 in the Cymru Premier Play-off final to qualify for the Uefa Conference League.

item.title: Birmingham, Palace promoted to WSL as Charlton fall to play-off
item.description: Birmingham City and Crystal Palace are promoted to the WSL after a dramatic final day in the second tier sees Charlton slip into the play-off spot.

item.title: Celtic reach Women's Scottish Cup final with extra-time win over Glasgow City
item.description: Celtic score twice in extra time to book their place in a first Women's Scottish Cup final in three years as they defeat Glasgow City for the first time since August 2024.

item.title: 'Shy' Anderson on England nerves and becoming a leader
item.description: Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson tells Kelly Somers about coming out of his shell to become a leader.

item.title: 'We got police escort to Vardy's party' - Leicester's 5,000-1 title win 10 years on
item.description: Premier League title-winning captain Wes Morgan and former Leicester City team-mate Marc Albrighton reflect on the club's 5,000-1 triumph a decade on.

item.title: 'I lost myself' - Bright on retirement, abuse and the future
item.description: Former England and Chelsea captain Millie Bright speaks to BBC Sport a day after announcing her retirement.

item.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
item.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.

item.title: How 10 Premier League teams can qualify for Europe
item.description: Ten Premier League teams in Europe? It sounds preposterous, but it is not as far-fetched as it seems.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Aberdeen ready to move for Hibs' Boyle - gossip
item.description: Aberdeen keen on experienced forward as free agent, Celtic keen on USA defender...

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Three into two - battle to reach WSL goes to final day
item.description: Just one matchday remains in the WSL 2 and Birmingham City, Crystal Palace and Charlton Athletic could all secure automatic promotion.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
